title: Baeria gracilis
taxonomy: Baeria gracilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Baeria, which is a group of plants native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. Baeria gracilis is a perennial herb with small, white or yellowish flowers. Its classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis.

intro: Baeria gracilis is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may contribute to its potential health benefits in reducing pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress.
